bits.name: rf_blk_cache_en0
bits.comment: 0: block 0 – cache disabled 1: block 0 – cache enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_cache_en1
bits.comment: 0: block 1 – cache disabled 1: block 1 – cache enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_cache_en2
bits.comment: 0: block 2 – cache disabled 1: block 2 – cache enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_cache_en3
bits.comment: 0: block 3 – cache disabled 1: block 3 – cache enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_cache_en4
bits.comment: 0: block 4 – cache disabled 1: block 4 – cache enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_cache_en5
bits.comment: 0: block 5 – cache disabled 1: block 5 – cache enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_cache_en6
bits.comment: 0: block 6 – cache disabled 1: block 6 – cache enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_cache_en7
bits.comment: 0: block 7 – cache disabled 1: block 7 – cache enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_remap_en0
bits.comment: 0: block 0 – cache remap disabled 1: block 0 – cache remap enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_remap_en1
bits.comment: 0: block 1 – cache remap disabled 1: block 1 – cache remap enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_remap_en2
bits.comment: 0: block 2 – cache remap disabled 1: block 2 – cache remap enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_remap_en3
bits.comment: 0: block 3 – cache remap disabled 1: block 3 – cache remap enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_remap_en4
bits.comment: 0: block 4 – cache remap disabled 1: block 4 – cache remap enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_remap_en5
bits.comment: 0: block 5 – cache remap disabled 1: block 5 – cache remap enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_remap_en6
bits.comment: 0: block 6 – cache remap disabled 1: block 6 – cache remap enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_remap_en7
bits.comment: 0: block 7 – cache remap disabled 1: block 7 – cache remap enabled

bits.name: rf_blk_prot_en0
bits.comment: 0: block 0 – protect disabled or access permitted 1: block 0 – protect enabled or access forbidden

bits.name: rf_blk_prot_en1
bits.comment: 0: block 1 – protect disabled or access permitted 1: block 1 – protect enabled or access forbidden

bits.name: rf_blk_prot_en2
bits.comment: 0: block 2 – protect disabled or access permitted 1: block 2 – protect enabled or access forbidden

bits.name: rf_blk_prot_en3
bits.comment: 0: block 3 – protect disabled or access permitted 1: block 3 – protect enabled or access forbidden

bits.name: rf_blk_prot_en4
bits.comment: 0: block 4 – protect disabled or access permitted 1: block 4 – protect enabled or access forbidden

bits.name: rf_blk_prot_en5
bits.comment: 0: block 5 – protect disabled or access permitted 1: block 5 – protect enabled or access forbidden

bits.name: rf_blk_prot_en6
bits.comment: 0: block 6 – protect disabled or access permitted 1: block 6 – protect enabled or access forbidden

bits.name: rf_blk_prot_en7
bits.comment: 0: block 7 – protect disabled or access permitted 1: block 7 – protect enabled or access forbidden

bits.name: rf_blk_base_addr1
bits.comment: block 1 start address

bits.name: rf_blk_base_addr2
bits.comment: block 2 start address

bits.name: rf_blk_base_addr3
bits.comment: block 3 start address

bits.name: rf_blk_base_addr4
bits.comment: block 4 start address

bits.name: rf_blk_base_addr5
bits.comment: block 5 start address

bits.name: rf_blk_base_addr6
bits.comment: block 6 start address

bits.name: rf_blk_base_addr7
bits.comment: block 7 start address

bits.name: rf_blk0_remap_offset
bits.comment: block 0 remap offset when the block 0 remap function is enabled. The address after remap will be original addr + rf_blk0_remap_offset

bits.name: rf_blk1_remap_offset
bits.comment: block 1 remap offset when the block 1 remap function is enabled. The address after remap will be original addr + rf_blk1_remap_offset

bits.name: rf_blk2_remap_offset
bits.comment: block 2 remap offset when the block 2 remap function is enabled. The address after remap will be original addr + rf_blk2_remap_offset

bits.name: rf_blk3_remap_offset
bits.comment: block 3 remap offset when the block 3 remap function is enabled. The address after remap will be original addr + rf_blk3_remap_offset

bits.name: rf_blk4_remap_offset
bits.comment: block 4 remap offset when the block 4 remap function is enabled. The address after remap will be original addr + rf_blk4_remap_offset

bits.name: rf_blk5_remap_offset
bits.comment: block 5 remap offset when the block 5 remap function is enabled. The address after remap will be original addr + rf_blk5_remap_offset

bits.name: rf_blk6_remap_offset
bits.comment: block 6 remap offset when the block 6 remap function is enabled. The address after remap will be original addr + rf_blk6_remap_offset

bits.name: rf_blk7_remap_offset
bits.comment: block 7 remap offset when the block 7 remap function is enabled. The address after remap will be original addr + rf_blk7_remap_offset

bits.name: rf_write_mode
bits.comment: cache write operation mode: 2’b00: write through 2’b01: write back, no write allocate 2’b10: write back, write allocate

bits.name: rf_hprot_byp
bits.comment: Bit [5]: 1’b1: hprot[3] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification 1’b0: hprot[3] is provided by cache controller register Bit [4]: 1’b1: hprot[2] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification 1’b0: hprot[2] is provided by cache controller register Bit [3]: 1’b1: hprot[1] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification 1’b0: hprot[1] is provided by cache controller register Bit [2]: 1’b1: hprot[0] from CM4 go through cache controller without modification 1’b0: hprot[0] is provided by cache controller register

bits.name: rf_hprot_bus_reg
bits.comment: hprot control register which provide 4bit hprot for cache bus AHB

bits.name: rf_hprot_cache_reg
bits.comment: hprot control register which provide 4bit hprot for cache ctrl AHB

bits.name: ahb_ram_en
bits.comment: Cache Data Ram Can be Write/Read Throught rf AHB Bus

bits.name: rf_cache_size_sel
bits.comment: cache size selection: 0: 4K Byte 1: 8K Byte 2: 16K Byte 3: 32K Byte

bits.name: rf_cmd_exec_mode
bits.comment: 0: (recommended) software can run in cacheable region during software command processing 1: software cannot run in cacheable region during software command processing

bits.name: rf_debug_en
bits.comment: cache debug mode enable: 0: normal mode 1: debug mode This bit MUST always be cleared during cache operating

bits.name: rf_prot_detect_addr
bits.comment: trigging address for protect block

bits.name: rf_cache_st
bits.comment: rf_cache_st

bits.name: rf_cmd_st
bits.comment: rf_cmd_st

bits.name: rf_write_ongoing
bits.comment: rf_write_ongoing, this is a status register for write through mode to avoid potential coherence issue. 1’b1: the cache is still doing AHB write transaction to the main memory and the data is not written into the main memory. 1’b0: the cache has finished AHB write transaction and the data is written into the main memory.

bits.name: rf_cmd_str_addr
bits.comment: cmd_all : not used cmd_range : start address cmd_entry : entry address

bits.name: rf_cmd_end_addr
bits.comment: cmd_all : not used cmd_range : end address cmd_entry : not used

bits.name: rf_cmd_type
bits.comment: software command type: 6’h0 : clean all 6’h1 : clean range 6’h2 : clean entry 6’h3 : reserved 6’h4 : invalid all 6’h5 : invalid range 6’h6 : invalid entry 6’h7 : reserved 6’h8 : clean and invalid all 6’h9 : clean and invalid range 6’hA : clean and invalid entry 6’hB : reserved

bits.name: rf_cmd_str
bits.comment: software command start: write 1 to this bit to issue one command

bits.name: rf_cmd_irq_en
bits.comment: interrupt enable for software command done

bits.name: rf_prot_irq_en
bits.comment: interrupt enable for protect block trigging

bits.name: rf_cmd_irq_raw
bits.comment: interrupt raw status for software command done

bits.name: rf_prot_irq_raw
bits.comment: interrupt raw status for protect block trigging

bits.name: rf_cmd_irq_mask
bits.comment: interrupt masked status for software command done

bits.name: rf_prot_irq_mask
bits.comment: interrupt masked status for protect block trigging

bits.name: rf_cmd_irq_clr
bits.comment: interrupt clear for software command done

bits.name: rf_prot_irq_clr
bits.comment: interrupt clear for protect block trigging

bits.name: rf_write_hit_cnt
bits.comment: Cache write hit times. When cache write hit, the counter value increment by 1

bits.name: rf_write_miss_cnt
bits.comment: Cache write miss times. When cache write miss, the counter value increment by 1

bits.name: rf_write_cnt_run
bits.comment: 1: write hit/miss counter will run 0: write hit/miss counter will stop

bits.name: rf_write_cnt_clr
bits.comment: clear write counter values to zero

bits.name: rf_read_hit_cnt
bits.comment: Cache read hit times. When cache read hit, the counter value increment by 1

bits.name: rf_read_miss_cnt
bits.comment: Cache read miss times. When cache read miss, the counter value increment by 1

bits.name: rf_read_cnt_run
bits.comment: 1: read hit/miss counter will run 0: read hit/miss counter will stop

bits.name: rf_read_cnt_clr
bits.comment: clear read counter values to zero

bits.name: rf_hact_cnt
bits.comment: Cache master AHB active cycles in total. When master AHB is active (hsel and htrans[1]), the counter value increment by 1

bits.name: rf_hrdy_cnt
bits.comment: The HRDY counter counts the valid cycles of HREADY signal from the cache controller to the master when the master AHB is active. When HREADY signal to the master is high and the master AHB is active, the counter value increment by 1

bits.name: rf_hact_hrdy_run
bits.comment: 1: HACT and HRDY counters will run 0: HACT and HRDY counters will stop

bits.name: rf_hact_hrdy_clr
bits.comment: clear HACT and HRDY counter values to zero
